notes: DOCUMENTED TARGET — NOT YET RECORDED (driver_gap:model-select). Two user turns in ONE live opencode `--session <id>` chain, separated by a MID-SESSION model swap: turn 1 runs on model A, the model is switched to model B, then turn 2 runs on model B — the same A→B mechanism the matrix's model-switch-midsession primitive asserts. THE MODEL SWITCH ITSELF IS NOT EXPRESSIBLE IN THIS DSL: the events.jsonl lifecycle stream the validator reads carries no model_name field (Phase has phase/expected_state/kind/max_delay_ms/duration_at_least_ms/same_session_as/new_session/invariants and nothing model-related). So this spec asserts only the TWO-TURN MID-SESSION LIFECYCLE SKELETON on ONE session — ready→working→ready twice on the SAME ses_… session_id, no waiting ever, PID bound, proving the switch happened DURING one live session in place (not via a restart). The model A→B change (ModelName follows latest-wins to model B; TotalTokens accumulates across the switch) is asserted out-of-band at the metrics-level summary.model_name in the .replay.json.golden (diffed by replay-fixtures), NOT this file. SESSION IDENTITY (opencode-specific): opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter (agent.go) — session state lives in the SQLite store and the daemon observes a UUID-keyed `ses_…` session; the driver chains turn 2 with `--session <id>` so both turns share one session record. session_birth is anchored to recording start and left UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession (if the process scanner emits one) cannot steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; turn1_start (matching working) establishes the session identity that the later turns chain off via same_session_as. WHY NOT RECORDABLE TODAY: the opencode interactive driver (drive-opencode-interactive.sh) drives headless `opencode run` and implements only send / wait_turn (no-op; `opencode run` blocks) / sleep — the `send` arm hardcodes the invocation with NO `--model` flag, so every turn uses the single config default model; there is NO per-step model-selection primitive and inline `/model` is a no-op on headless `opencode run` (proven by a live store probe — see assessment.json). Mid-session model B is therefore unreachable via the send path. This spec is retained as the recordable target once the driver gains a model-select step (a developer task); see replaydata/agents/opencode/scenarios/model-switch-midsession/assessment.json (driver_capability: gap:model-select). HOW runs differ (once recorded): concrete model values are environment-dependent (issue #440 — assert presence/change, not a hardcoded pair); assistant wording, per-turn token counts, the ses_… UUID/PID, and sub-second timing vary. Stable structurally: two ready→working→ready arcs on ONE ses_… session, no waiting, a final ready state — plus (golden-level, not here) a non-empty summary.model_name equal to model B.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of agent launch (the ses_… store session); never enters waiting (no tool use)
PID bound to the ses_… session
First prompt drives the ses_… session ready → working on model A
First turn completes back to ready on model A, same ses_… session; state never entered waiting
After the mid-session model switch, the second prompt drives the SAME ses_… session ready → working, now on model B
Second turn completes back to ready on model B, same ses_… session; state never entered waiting
